tracks.meaning: the heaviness of a person or object, typically measured in units such as pounds or kilograms
the force exerted by gravity on a mass, equal to mass times gravitational acceleration
the significance, influence, or importance given to something in a decision or consideration
a financial burden, obligation, or debt that affects a person or organization
a heavy object lifted in weightlifting competitions or training exercises
the thickness or density of a fabric, affecting how it drapes and feels
the thickness or boldness of a typeface or font style
an emotional or mental burden that affects a person's mood or wellbeing
to add heaviness to something or to make something heavier
a numerical coefficient assigned to data points to reflect their relative importance in a calculation
a numerical parameter in a machine learning model that affects the influence of input features
the relative importance or credibility given to evidence or testimony in a legal proceeding
to alter odds or probabilities in favor of a particular outcome through manipulation
ballast or heavy material placed in a vessel to improve stability and balance
something burdensome, oppressive, or difficult to endure
